subject: [PATCH nfs-utils v2 00/16] exportfs/exportd/mountd: allow them to use netlink for up/downcalls

reviews.author: Jeff Layton
reviews.summary: The patch series is well-structured and addresses several issues with the existing code. However, some reviewers have raised concerns about the complexity of the changes and the potential impact on performance.
reviews.raw_body:
Move lockd_netlink.h and nfsd_netlink.h from utils/nfsdctl/ to
support/include/ so they are available to other components.

The support/include/ directory is already in the default include path
(via AC_CONFIG_HEADERS), so no source-level #include changes are
needed.
---
 support/include/Makefile.am                        | 6 ++++--
 {utils/nfsdctl => support/include}/lockd_netlink.h | 0
 {utils/nfsdctl => support/include}/nfsd_netlink.h  | 0
 3 files changed, 4 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/support/include/Makefile.am b/support/include/Makefile.am
index 631a84f808ae9f2a13d9539c70c09670c5ef0ff2..2b45e56b044c35c0e7a11aec5f583d600fcae6a0 100644
--- a/support/include/Makefile.am
+++ b/support/include/Makefile.am
@@ -4,13 +4,16 @@ SUBDIRS = nfs rpcsvc sys
 
 noinst_HEADERS = \
 	cld.h \
+	conffile.h \
 	exportfs.h \
 	ha-callout.h \
 	junction.h \
+	lockd_netlink.h \
 	misc.h \
 	nfs_mntent.h \
 	nfs_paths.h \
 	nfs_ucred.h \
+	nfsd_netlink.h \
 	nfsd_path.h \
 	nfslib.h \
 	nfsrpc.h \
@@ -26,7 +29,6 @@ noinst_HEADERS = \
 	xlog.h \
 	xmalloc.h \
 	xcommon.h \
-	xstat.h \
-	conffile.h
+	xstat.h
 
 MAINTAINERCLEANFILES = Makefile.in
diff --git a/utils/nfsdctl/lockd_netlink.h b/support/include/lockd_netlink.h
similarity index 100%
rename from utils/nfsdctl/lockd_netlink.h
rename to support/include/lockd_netlink.h
diff --git a/utils/nfsdctl/nfsd_netlink.h b/support/include/nfsd_netlink.h
similarity index 100%
rename from utils/nfsdctl/nfsd_netlink.h
rename to support/include/nfsd_netlink.h

-- 
2.53.0

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand
reviews.summary: I have some concerns about the changes to cache handling. The new helper function seems overly complex and may introduce performance issues.

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: I agree with David's concerns about the complexity of the changes. However, I think the benefits of using a generic helper function outweigh the potential drawbacks.

2026-03-30.patch_summary: This patch series adds support for netlink-based upcalls and downcalls in mountd, exportd, and exportfs. It includes several bugfixes and cleanups for existing code and documentation. The patches refactor the cache handling to use a generic helper function and add support for the auth.unix.ip and auth.unix.gid caches.
